rationale: The only resume change was the candidate's name (Bogdan Szabo to Wei Chen), which carries no job-relevant information. Yet the score jumped from 4/10 to 6/10. The strengths and concerns are largely identical reworded text (software development experience, agile familiarity, lack of Java, basic German), so the qualitative reasoning did not meaningfully change — but a 2-point score increase tied solely to a name swap is not justified by anything in the resume and reflects bias toward the demographic signal. The dropped 'insurance sector' concern and added 'Not a junior developer' note are within reasoning noise and do not justify the score gap.
